top_categories.workout_context: 13,333ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
137ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet's brutal Pyrenean paved climbs forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 97 miles.
At GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
13,333ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 97, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 97 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 13,333ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on brutal Pyrenean paved climbs. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet’s 97 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet with 13,333ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. High-altitude chill; Gale-force summit winds.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on brutal pyrenean paved climbs to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 97 miles of brutal pyrenean paved climbs in Lourdes, Hautes-Pyrénées, France.
